title: Northveil Energy's Green Bond Offering Collapses After ESG Downgrade
body:
Northveil Energy Partners pulled a $500 million green bond offering late Friday after Sustain Ratings, a leading ESG assessment firm, downgraded the Houston-based company's environmental score from A- to C+ — a move that would have barred several large institutional buyers from participating.

Northveil had planned to use the proceeds to finance a wind farm expansion in West Texas and retire older coal-adjacent generation assets in its portfolio. The bond was being underwritten by three banks and was expected to price on Thursday.

The downgrade was triggered by a Sustain Ratings investigation into Northveil's methane monitoring practices at its natural gas gathering operations in the Permian Basin. The firm cited what it called "material discrepancies" between Northveil's self-reported emissions figures and satellite-based measurements published by an environmental research group in February.

Northveil's chief financial officer, Warren Gault, called the downgrade "premature and methodologically flawed" in a statement Friday evening. The company said it would seek an expedited review.

The collapse rattled the green bond market, which has faced mounting skepticism from investors over the reliability of ESG ratings. Halcyon Genomics, which holds a small green bond portfolio as part of its treasury management strategy, was among the institutional investors that had quietly indicated interest in the Northveil offering, according to a person familiar with the matter.
